commands.review_summary:
The PR modifies only `bun.lock` to refresh Astro/Vite/Rollup/esbuild-related dependency resolutions for security advisories.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a dependency-security PR rather than a runtime bug report. Source inspection does show current main locks the older vulnerable versions and the PR head moves the targeted entries forward.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof and maintainer acceptance of the partial esbuild security posture are needed; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.

target.title: fix(deps): refresh vulnerable build dependencies
target.body:
Refresh `bun.lock` to pick up patched transitive dependencies. The old lockfile pinned stale versions with known vulnerabilities.

## Resolved

| Package | Old | New | Advisory |
|---|---|---|---|
| fast-xml-builder | 1.1.5 | 1.2.0 | CVE-2026-44665 |
| rollup | 4.55.1 | 4.60.4 | CVE-2026-27606 |
| vite | 7.3.2 | 7.3.5 | CVE-2026-53571 |

The previously cited esbuild advisory, GHSA-gv7w-rqvm-qjhr, was withdrawn by GitHub on June 17, 2026 because the affected package was misidentified.

## Verification

- `bun install --frozen-lockfile`
- `bun test` (10 tests)
- `bun run test:built-assets` (20-page Astro build plus asset compatibility checks)
- Codex autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings

Verified against the PR head merged with current `main` on June 19, 2026.
target.author: sebtardif
